leagueBriefing: Michigan clinched a perfect 15-0 finish to the Big Ten season by sweeping rival Michigan State, while mid-major tournaments saw massive upsets as top seeds Navy and Marshall both went down at the buzzer.

games.headline: Mirkovic helps No. 11 Illinois clinch Big Ten bye in gritty road win
games.brief: Illinois survived a serious scare in College Park to lock up a crucial double-bye for the Big Ten Tournament. Despite a dismal shooting night from deep, the Illini relied on David Mirkovic’s interior presence and a late 7-0 run to fend off a Maryland team playing for pride in a disappointing season.
bullets: David Mirkovic led the charge with a double-double, tallying 22 points and 11 rebounds.
The Illini tied a program record with their eighth Big Ten road win of the season.
Maryland's Andre Mills poured in 30 points in the loss as the Terps fell to a 20-loss season for the first time since 1989.
Illinois secured the win despite shooting a frigid 5-of-24 from three-point range.
games.context: The win guarantees Illinois a spot in Friday's Big Ten quarterfinals; Maryland enters the tournament as a No. 17 seed.

games.headline: Lendeborg leads No. 3 Michigan to rivalry sweep and 15th straight win
games.brief: In a high-stakes rivalry matchup, No. 3 Michigan asserted its dominance over Michigan State to cap off an incredible 15-game conference winning streak. Yaxel Lendeborg was the catalyst, exploding for 27 points to ensure the Big Ten champions entered the postseason with maximum momentum, while the Spartans struggled with both the score and their composure.
bullets: Yaxel Lendeborg scored 19 of his 27 points in the first half and matched a career high with five 3-pointers.
Michigan has now won 15 consecutive Big Ten games, finishing the regular season 29-2.
Spartans guard Jeremy Fears Jr. was assessed a technical foul after a video review showed him kicking an opponent.
Jaxon Kohler matched his career high with 23 points for Michigan State in the losing effort.
games.context: Michigan cements its case for a No. 1 seed in the NCAA Tournament; both teams head to the Big Ten quarterfinals Friday.

games.headline: Jacobsen’s overtime heroics help No. 9 Nebraska match program wins record
games.brief: Pinnacle Bank Arena was rocking as Nebraska survived an Iowa comeback to secure their 26th win of the season, matching an all-time school record. After the Hawkeyes forced overtime with a late 3-pointer, bench spark Cale Jacobsen took over in the extra period to ensure a sentimental Senior Day victory for Sam Hoiberg and his father, coach Fred Hoiberg.
bullets: Cale Jacobsen scored 13 of his 15 points after halftime, including the tiebreaking three in overtime.
Sam Hoiberg celebrated Senior Day with 15 points and 5 steals.
Nebraska tied the program record for most wins in a single season (26).
Kael Combs led Iowa with 18 points, including the clutch triple to force overtime with 2 seconds left.
games.context: Nebraska clinches the No. 2 seed in the Big Ten Tournament; Iowa will start as the No. 9 seed.

games.headline: Alvey’s 30-point explosion sends Lehigh to Patriot League title game
games.brief: Hank Alvey was simply too much for Colgate to handle as he powered Lehigh into the Patriot League Championship game. Behind Alvey’s 30 points and a lockdown defensive performance from Nasir Whitlock, the Mountain Hawks ended Colgate's season and set themselves up for a shot at the NCAA Tournament bid on Wednesday.
bullets: Hank Alvey dominated with 30 points, 5 rebounds, and 3 blocks.
Nasir Whitlock recorded a double-double with 22 points and 10 assists.
Lehigh used a 16-2 first-half run to take control of the momentum.
Jalen Cox led Colgate with 26 points, but the Raiders couldn't overcome Lehigh's interior scoring.
games.context: Lehigh advances to the Patriot League championship game on Wednesday against Boston University.

games.headline: Rutgers smothers Penn State early to complete regular season sweep
games.brief: Rutgers finished the regular season on a high note, using a suffocating 16-0 first-half run to bury Penn State early. Tariq Francis and Lino Mark provided the offensive punch, but it was the Scarlet Knights' defense that told the story, holding the Nittany Lions to a disastrous 3-of-22 shooting performance from beyond the arc.
bullets: Tariq Francis led Rutgers with 18 points, including three 3-pointers.
Rutgers blew the game open early with a 16-0 run that resulted in a 37-14 lead.
Darren Buchanan Jr. dominated the glass with 12 rebounds.
Penn State struggled from deep, shooting just 13.6% (3-of-22) from three-point range.
games.context: Rutgers sweeps the season series and heads to Chicago for the Big Ten Tournament starting Tuesday.

games.headline: Gladden’s buzzer-beater stuns top-seeded Navy and ends 14-game streak
games.brief: In an absolute heartbreaker in Annapolis, Chance Gladden played the role of giant-killer. After top-seeded Navy took the lead with only three seconds left, Gladden raced down the court and buried a 3-pointer at the buzzer to snap the Midshipmen's 14-game winning streak and send the fourth-seeded Terriers to the Patriot League championship.
bullets: Chance Gladden scored 24 of his 26 points in the second half, capped by the buzzer-beating winner.
The upset ended Navy’s 14-game winning streak and their hope for a top-seed tourney title.
BU's Michael McNair added 22 points, hitting five 3-pointers.
Aidan Kehoe led Navy in the loss with a massive 26-point, 12-rebound double-double.
games.context: Boston University advances to the Patriot League final to face Lehigh on Wednesday.

games.headline: Berry's clutch free throws in OT snap Memphis' seven-game skid
games.brief: Memphis finally found their way back into the win column after a grueling overtime battle in New Orleans. Quante Berry was the hero for the Tigers, drawing a foul and sinking the winning free throws with just two seconds left to survive a career-high 31-point performance from Tulane’s Rowan Brumbaugh.
bullets: Quante Berry led Memphis with 22 points, including the winning free throws with 2.3 seconds left in OT.
Rowan Brumbaugh scored a career-high 31 points for Tulane in the heartbreak.
Memphis snapped a seven-game losing streak with the victory.
Dug McDaniel contributed 20 points, 7 rebounds, and 6 assists for the Tigers.
games.context: Memphis ends the regular season with a win to build momentum for the AAC Tournament.

games.headline: Monmouth uses late 11-0 run to bounce Drexel from CAA tourney
games.brief: Monmouth kept their NCAA Tournament hopes alive by locking down Drexel in the second half of the CAA quarterfinals. Trailing midway through the period, the Hawks unleashed an 11-0 blitz to take the lead for good, powered by double-digit scoring from Jason Rivera-Torres and Stefanos Spartalis.
bullets: Jason Rivera-Torres scored 11 of his 14 points in the second half.
Monmouth outscored Drexel by 9 points in the second half to erase a halftime deficit.
Stefanos Spartalis added 14 points on efficient 5-of-7 shooting.
Kevon Vanderhorst led Drexel with 15 points in their season-ending loss.
games.context: Monmouth advances to the CAA semifinals to play Campbell on Monday.

games.headline: Green and Barnstable help Tulsa survive Temple in regular season finale
games.brief: Tulsa ended their regular season on a high note by surviving a back-and-forth thriller against Temple. In a game that saw nine lead changes in the second half alone, the Golden Hurricane leaned on David Green’s scoring and some clutch perimeter shooting from Miles Barnstable to hold off the Owls' upset bid.
bullets: David Green paced Tulsa with 22 points and 6 rebounds.
The second half alone featured 9 lead changes and 7 ties.
Temple's Derrian Ford led all scorers with 23 points in the narrow loss.
Miles Barnstable hit three key 3-pointers, finishing with 11 of his 13 points after halftime.
games.context: The American Athletic Conference Tournament begins Wednesday.

games.headline: Smith's hot start carries Rice past UTSA to close AAC season
games.brief: Rice never trailed after the opening minutes as Jalen Smith took over early against UTSA. The Owls built a double-digit halftime cushion and kept the Roadrunners at arm's length throughout the second half, ensuring they enter the conference tournament with a victory.
bullets: Jalen Smith led the Owls with 25 points and 6 rebounds.
Rice took the lead at the 17:48 mark of the first half and held it the rest of the way.
Nick Anderson contributed 20 points for Rice on efficient shooting.
UTSA's Matheo Coffi recorded a double-double with 12 points and 14 rebounds.
games.context: Rice closes the AAC regular season 7-11 heading into tournament play Wednesday.

games.headline: Westry’s double-double powers UAB past ECU in regular season finale
games.brief: UAB locked in their 20th win of the season by overcoming a halftime deficit to defeat East Carolina. Chance Westry was the engine of the Blazers' offense, facilitating at an elite level with 11 assists while matching Daniel Rivera for the team lead in scoring to secure the win in front of the Birmingham crowd.
bullets: Chance Westry recorded 22 points and 11 assists for his second straight double-double.
Daniel Rivera matched Westry with 22 points for the Blazers.
UAB finished the regular season with a 20-11 record (11-7 in AAC).
ECU’s Demitri Gardner led the Pirates with 19 points in the loss.
games.context: UAB enters the AAC Tournament as the No. 4 seed on their home court this Wednesday.

games.headline: Tejada’s 30-point masterclass fuels Towson’s upset romp over Charleston
games.brief: Towson didn't just beat second-seeded Charleston; they dismantled them. In the shock of the CAA Tournament so far, Tyler Tejada exploded for 30 points as the 7th-seeded Tigers jumped out to an early lead and never let up, using a massive second-half run to turn a competitive game into a total blowout.
bullets: Tyler Tejada shot 11-of-17 from the field for 30 points.
Towson used a 13-0 run in the second half to extend their lead to 24 points.
The Tigers shot lights-out from deep, led by Ryan Conway and Tejada.
Charleston, the No. 2 seed, was held to just 56 points in their early exit.
games.context: No. 7 seed Towson advances to Monday's CAA semifinals after an emphatic upset.

games.headline: Bellamy’s defensive clinic leads top-seeded Troy into Sun Belt final
games.brief: Top-seeded Troy took a major step toward the Big Dance, fending off a resilient Southern Miss squad in the Sun Belt semifinals. Jerrell Bellamy was the story on both ends of the floor, pairing his 22 points with a staggering 6 blocks to anchor the Trojans' defense against a career-high effort from Southern Miss star Isaac Tavares.
bullets: Jerrell Bellamy posted 22 points and a season-high 6 blocks.
Southern Miss’s Isaac Tavares scored a game-high 32 points in the loss.
Troy held the lead for the final 33 minutes of the game.
Trojans reserve Cooper Campbell added 14 critical second-half points.
games.context: Troy advances to Monday's Sun Belt Championship game with an NCAA bid on the line.

games.headline: Dorset’s late triple sends top-seeded Merrimack to MAAC title game
games.brief: Merrimack survived a defensive grind in Atlantic City thanks to some late-game heroics from Tye Dorset. In a game that went down to the final possession, Dorset's go-ahead 3-pointer with 85 seconds remaining proved to be the difference, keeping the Warriors' dream of a MAAC title and NCAA Tournament berth alive.
bullets: Tye Dorset scored a game-high 23 points, including the winning 3-pointer with 1:25 left.
Merrimack's defense held Marist scoreless over the final 1:25 of play.
Kevair Kennedy added 16 points and 3 steals for the Warriors.
Marist was led by Rhyjon Blackwell with 15 points in the heartbreaker.
games.context: No. 1 seed Merrimack will play for the MAAC title on Tuesday.

games.headline: Wilkins explodes for 34 as Furman rallies to reach SoCon final
games.brief: Furman is one win away from the NCAA Tournament after a sensational second-half performance from Alex Wilkins. Trailing by 11 at the half, the Paladins rode Wilkins' hot hand as he poured in 28 of his 34 points after the break to surge past UNC Greensboro in the Southern Conference semifinals.
bullets: Alex Wilkins scored 34 points, with 28 coming in the second half alone.
Furman overcame a 38-27 halftime deficit with a 54-point second-half outburst.
Justin Neely led the Spartans with 27 points in the loss.
The Paladins shot a high percentage in the second half to erase UNCG's lead.
games.context: Furman (No. 6 seed) will face No. 1 seed ETSU for the SoCon championship on Monday.

games.headline: Davis powers Hofstra to 31-point demolition of William & Mary
games.brief: Hofstra left no doubt in their CAA quarterfinal matchup, crushing William & Mary from the opening tip. Cruz Davis was the star of the show, racking up 30 points and leading an offensive explosion that saw the Pride build a 17-point halftime lead and never look back in a total dominant performance.
bullets: Cruz Davis led all scorers with 30 points and added 5 assists.
Hofstra took the lead at 18:33 of the first half and never trailed again.
The Pride shot effectively from all levels, building a lead as large as 33 points.
Jo'el Emanuel led the Tribe with 16 points and 8 rebounds in their season finale.
games.context: No. 3 seed Hofstra advances to face Towson in the CAA semifinals on Monday.

games.headline: Shoats and Doty combine for 51 as Siena punches ticket to MAAC final
games.brief: Siena is headed to the MAAC championship after Justice Shoats and Gavin Doty put on a scoring clinic against Fairfield. The Saints took control mid-way through the first half and used a late 10-0 run to extinguish any hope of a Fairfield comeback, setting up a heavyweight title bout against top-seeded Merrimack.
bullets: Justice Shoats scored 26 points, including a perfect 3-for-3 from deep.
Gavin Doty added 25 points and 8 rebounds, going 10-of-11 from the foul line.
Siena dominated the second half, leading by as many as 15 points down the stretch.
Declan Wucherpfennig recorded a double-double (13 pts, 10 reb) for Fairfield.
games.context: No. 3 seed Siena will play No. 1 seed Merrimack for the MAAC title and an NCAA berth on Tuesday.

games.headline: Spudd Webb sparks massive upset as 10-seed Georgia Southern reaches Sun Belt final
games.brief: The Cinderella story of the Sun Belt Tournament continues. No. 10 seed Georgia Southern shocked second-seeded Marshall behind a 31-point outburst from Spudd Webb. The Eagles have now won five straight games in the tournament and are just one win away from one of the most improbable NCAA Tournament berths in history.
bullets: Spudd Webb scored 31 points, including 18 in the second half to seal the upset.
Georgia Southern is the No. 10 seed and has won 5 games in 5 days.
Andres Burney contributed a double-double with 12 points and 11 rebounds.
Marshall's Shamarrie Hugie led the Thundering Herd with 19 points in the loss.
games.context: Georgia Southern will face No. 1 seed Troy in the championship on Monday.

games.headline: Brickner’s perfect second half fuels Idaho’s upset of Montana State
games.brief: Idaho pulled off a stunning second-half turnaround to bounce second-seeded Montana State from the Big Sky Tournament. After going scoreless in the first half, Isaiah Brickner went a perfect 6-of-8 from the floor after the break to lead the Vandals' charge, as they erased a halftime deficit to secure their spot in the semifinals.
bullets: Isaiah Brickner scored all 15 of his points in the second half.
Biko Johnson led the Vandals with 19 points, 5 rebounds, and 4 assists.
No. 7 seed Idaho took the lead for good with five minutes remaining.
Jeremiah Davis led Montana State with 22 points in the defeat.
games.context: No. 7 seed Idaho advances to the Big Sky semifinals on Tuesday.

games.headline: Graves sparks Santa Clara off the bench to reach WCC semifinals
games.brief: Santa Clara is moving on in Las Vegas after a dominant performance from reserve Allen Graves. The Broncos used a blistering 12-0 run early in the second half to separate from Pacific, maintaining a comfortable lead despite a late flurry of three-pointers from Tigers star Elias Ralph.
bullets: Allen Graves came off the bench to score 19 points on 7-of-10 shooting.
Santa Clara used a 12-0 second-half run to build a 14-point lead.
Elijah Mahi anchored the interior with 13 points and 11 rebounds.
Pacific’s Elias Ralph led all scorers with 25 points, including five 3-pointers.
games.context: No. 3 seed Santa Clara advances to play No. 2 seed Saint Mary's in Monday's semifinal.
